Label: purpose
Rationale: Useful for learning metric to imperial conversion and nearest fraction rounding

Label: backend,frontend
Text: Divisor shall convert metric values to imperial

Label: backend,frontend
Text: Divisor shall round decimal values to the nearest fraction of an inch

Label: backend,frontend
Text: Divisor shall round decimal values to the nearest fraction of a foot+inch

Label: backend,frontend
Text: Divisor shall offer even numbered divisors for fractions
Rationale: 1/8, 1/16, 1/32, and 1/64 are the most common divisors for imperial measurements; 1/4 removed to make room for the C (clear all) button

Label: backend,frontend
Text: Divisor should offer an auto divisor for fractions
Rationale: Based on the decimal returns, round to the nearest rational divisor as specified in Requirement #4

Label: backend,frontend
Text: Divisor shall be deployed as a website
Rationale: Github pages offers a simple, integrated hosting service

Label: frontend
Text: Divisor website shall be responsive, adapting to both mobile and desktop screen resolutions

Label: frontend
Text: Mobile Divisor users shall be able to input values with numeric keyboard

Label: frontend
Text: Divisor website shall offer dynamic interactions

Label: backend,frontend
Text: Divisor shall evaluate basic math expressions in any input field
Rationale: Allows users to enter expressions like 12/4 before converting; guards against division by zero and negative results

Label: frontend
Text: Divisor shall display rounding error as +decimal beside each result
Rationale: Shows e.g. +0.038 when rounding occurs; blank when exact

Label: frontend
Text: Divisor shall not overflow input or output fields for large values on mobile
Rationale: All field content shall remain fully visible and within bounds regardless of input magnitude

Label: frontend
Text: Divisor shall display a log of recent inputs
Rationale: Shows timestamp active divisor field name and raw input value for each settled entry; debounced to avoid logging every keystroke; C button clears the log
